item.title: 🇯🇵 Developed Asia draws private capital as investors pivot away from China
item.description: Japan is leading a shift in private capital allocation as LPs prioritize exits, stronger governance, and proven returns, with Australia and Korea also gaining attention from investors seeking alternatives to China exposure.

item.title: 🇷🇺 Russia has finally turned its back on Europe, Berliner Zeitung reports
item.description: German newspaper Berliner Zeitung writes that Russia has reoriented itself to China, with EU-Russia trade shrinking from nearly $400 billion in 2013 to just $64 billion by 2025, an 80% decline since 2021.

item.title: 🇹🇷 Erdogan tells Trump Iran issues can be resolved as Turkey mediates
item.description: Turkish President Erdogan told Trump he welcomed the ceasefire extension with Iran and believes contested issues can be resolved. Turkey has been passing messages between Washington, Tehran, and mediators Pakistan.

item.title: 🇹🇷 Turkey's parliament unites to demand US extradite FETO leader Gulen
item.description: Turkey's governing and opposition parties issued a joint declaration calling for the extradition of Fetullah Gulen, the US-based leader of FETO, which Turkey accuses of orchestrating the 2016 coup attempt that killed 251 people.

item.title: 🇨🇺 US indicts former Cuban leader Raúl Castro over 1996 plane shootdown
item.description: The US Justice Department charged Castro with murder and conspiracy for ordering the downing of two civilian planes operated by Miami-based exile group Brothers to the Rescue, killing four people including three Americans. The indictment marks a sharp escalation in US pressure on Cuba's communist government.

item.title: ✈️ US military surveillance flights track movements near Cuba amid tensions
item.description: BBC Verify analysis shows US Navy P-8A Poseidon jets and MQ-4C Triton drones have been operating near Cuba since May 11, with some flights coming within 50 miles of the island. The public nature of the flights signals US intent to enforce the oil blockade and pressure Havana.

item.title: 🇷🇺 Russian jets dangerously intercept British spy plane over Black Sea
item.description: The UK Defence Ministry said two Russian Su-35 and Su-27 aircraft repeatedly intercepted an unarmed RAF Rivet Joint surveillance aircraft in international airspace in April, coming within six metres of the plane. Britain called it the most dangerous Russian action against a UK aircraft since 2022.

item.title: 📈 Iran reopens stock market after three-month war closure
item.description: Tehran Stock Exchange resumed trading with restrictions after being shut since February. About 36% of companies remain suspended due to war damage, while inflation exceeds 70% and the rial continues to slide against the dollar.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 Trump Says Iran Talks on Borderline as Tehran Reviews US Proposal
item.description: US President Donald Trump said negotiations with Iran are on the borderline between a deal and further military action, suggesting Washington could wait a few days for the right answer from Tehran.

item.title: 🇬🇧⛽ UK Extends Fuel Duty Cut to Ease Iran War Inflation Pressure
item.description: Britain will extend a cut in motor fuel duty until the end of the year as the government seeks to ease cost-of-living pressures heightened by the Middle East conflict and rising global energy costs.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇿🦠 US Doctor Exposed to Ebola Transferred to Czech Hospital from Uganda
item.description: A US doctor who came into contact with an Ebola patient will be transferred from Uganda to a hospital in Prague for monitoring, Czech health officials said.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇩🇪🏥 American Ebola Patient in Stable Condition in German Hospital
item.description: A US citizen who contracted Ebola in the Democratic Republic of Congo is being treated in Germany and is in stable condition, the CDC said, as the rare Bundibugyo strain outbreak continues.

item.title: 🇮🇷⚔️ Iranian public receives gun training as war fears mount
item.description: Revolutionary Guard members are teaching civilians to handle assault rifles in Tehran, with weapons displays and missiles featured at public events amid Trump threats to restart the war.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇬🇱 US envoy pushes to reestablish military presence in Greenland
item.description: US special envoy Jeff Landry says Washington needs to put its footprint back on Greenland, with plans for three new bases as Trump pursues control of the Arctic territory.

item.title: 🇱🇧⛪ South Lebanon cultural heritage destroyed amid Israeli buffer zone creation
item.description: Lebanese activists document widespread demolition of religious structures and historic sites as Israel establishes a forward defence zone encompassing 55 villages since April ceasefire.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Consolidates Control of Strait of Hormuz with Island Checkpoints
item.description: Iran has established a multi-tiered checkpoint system for vessel clearance through the Strait of Hormuz, using diplomatic deals and sometimes fees. The US has warned against complying with Iranian controls as some shippers and governments take the risk.

item.title: 🦠 Ebola Outbreak Response Hindered by US Aid Cuts Under Trump
item.description: Aid cuts by the Trump administration have shut down crucial disease surveillance networks and medical supply chains in East Africa, potentially hampering efforts to contain the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo.

item.title: 🇱🇹 Lithuanian Lawmakers Shelter as Drone Violates Airspace Near Belarus Border
item.description: Residents of Vilnius were told to take shelter and Lithuanian leaders were moved to safe locations after a drone violated airspace near the Belarus border, highlighting NATO eastern flank security concerns.

item.title: 🇬🇧 Bank of England Has Time to Gauge Iran War Economic Impact
item.description: A rise in market interest rates since the start of the Iran war has given the Bank of England more time to assess the economic impact of the conflict, according to Governor Andrew Bailey.

item.title: 🇸🇦 Saudi Arabia Crude Exports Drop to Record Low in March
item.description: Saudi Arabia's crude oil exports fell to a record low of 4.974 million barrels per day in March, the lowest level since data collection began in 2002, according to JODI data.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇷🇺 Xi and Putin hail growing China-Russia ties in Beijing meeting
item.description: Chinese leader Xi Jinping and Russian President Vladimir Putin oversaw the signing of 40+ cooperation agreements and declared their partnership at its highest level in history, extending a 2001 friendship treaty.

item.title: 🦠 Rare Ebola type spreads rapidly in eastern Congo
item.description: Healthcare workers warn they are underprepared as the Bundibugyo virus Ebola outbreak spreads in Congo, with 51 confirmed cases and 139 suspected deaths amid conflict zones.

item.title: 🕵️ Austrian ex-intelligence officer found guilty of Russia spying charges
item.description: Former Austrian intelligence official Egisto Ott was sentenced to four years for passing sensitive information to Russian intelligence and Wirecard fugitive Jan Marsalek.

item.title: 🇨🇺 US indicts former Cuban president Raúl Castro over 1996 plane downing
item.description: The US Justice Department charged the 94-year-old former Cuban leader with conspiracy to kill US nationals and murder for the 1996 shooting down of two humanitarian planes that killed four people.

item.title: 🇹🇼 Taiwan President Lai Asserts Only Taiwan Can Decide Its Future
item.description: President Lai Ching-te stated Taiwan's future must not be decided by external forces and cannot be held hostage by fear, drawing an angry reaction from Beijing.
